topics.title: Summer House 'Scandoval 2.0': Amanda Batula + West Wilson vs. Ciara Miller
topics.hook: Kyle Cooke alleges West had a secret girlfriend the whole time — 'In The City' spinoff premieres May 19
topics.summary: Amanda Batula and Kyle Cooke split in January 2026. Amanda quickly confirmed a romance with West Wilson, Ciara Miller's ex and her best friend. The reunion generated massive Bravo discourse about whether the Kyle-Amanda marriage was ever truly romantic — organically surfacing lavender marriage discourse in comment sections.
topics.relevance: Partnership-of-convenience vs. romantic love framing — perfect LLMA entry point, already covered May 6 and May 11.
topics.note: Skipped as primary — covered in the last two drops. Strong as a supporting reference.

topics.title: Lavender Marriages Are Going Mainstream: Gen Z Women Are Openly Applying
topics.hook: A single TikTok about marrying a gay best friend hit 3.6M views and 600K comments — mostly from straight women saying 'yes'
topics.summary: The #lavendermarriage hashtag is surging on TikTok in 2026, driven by Gen Z women and gay men openly discussing intentional platonic marriages. The trend is fueled by dating fatigue, economic pressures, LGBTQ+ rights anxiety, and a desire for safe, respectful companionship. Unlike the historical closeted version, this iteration is transparent and consensual.
topics.relevance: Direct topic match — the conversation is happening at massive scale without authoritative framing. LLMA can own this.

topics.title: Whitney Leavitt Keeps Denying Lavender Marriage Rumors — and Nobody Believes Her
topics.hook: Secret Lives of Mormon Wives star vs. the internet's most stubborn theory
topics.summary: Whitney Leavitt has spent over a year publicly denying that her marriage to Conner is a lavender arrangement. Conner's alleged Grindr activity fueled speculation. Her responses — while earnest — have kept 'lavender marriage' circulating in mainstream pop culture for audiences who never knew the term.
topics.relevance: Mainstream on-ramp for the term. Used as pop culture hook in today's drop.
